meaning: adj.存在的,生存的,现存的n.存在的事物,生存者
examples: The self is the only knowable existent.
This policy, they say, is at best confused and at worst non-existent.
Manners nowadays in metropolitan cities like London are practically non-existent.
But androgen receptors were found to be nearly non-existent in the brains of newborn animals.
Growth in the West simply had become extremely low, or in Europe, on balance, non-existent.
